channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: U.S. strikes Iran in response to a drone attack on a ship
item.description: The U.S. struck Iran on Friday in response to a drone attack a day earlier on a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz. It's the most significant test yet to an interim understanding reached a week ago by the two countries.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. struck Iran on Friday in response to a drone attack a day earlier on a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz. It's the most significant test yet to an interim understanding reached a week ago by the two countries.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Pete Buttigieg and his kids subject to CPS, police investigation after false report
item.description: Buttigieg, a potential 2028 Democratic presidential contender, shared his family's experience when police and Child Protective Services came to his home investigating an anonymous tip that was false.
item.content:encoded:
Buttigieg, a potential 2028 Democratic presidential contender, shared his family's experience when police and Child Protective Services came to his home investigating an anonymous tip that was false.

(Image credit: Drew Angerer)
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore

item.title: 5 million have dropped ACA insurance after Trump and the GOP let prices skyrocket
item.description: The number of people who signed up for a health plan and then didn't pay their premium fell much more sharply than it has in years past. Trump officials blame fraud. Health policy experts blame costs.
item.content:encoded:
The number of people who signed up for a health plan and then didn't pay their premium fell much more sharply than it has in years past. Trump officials blame fraud. Health policy experts blame costs.

(Image credit: Patrick Sison)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: A day after Alito's testy response to Sotomayor's dissent, court says it was a 'misunderstanding'
item.description: During Supreme Court opinions Thursday, Justice Samuel Alito, who wrote the majority opinion in an asylum case, appeared to rebut Justice Sonia Sotomayor, who wrote the dissent. The move was highly unusual — and on Friday there was a coda.
item.content:encoded:
During Supreme Court opinions Thursday, Justice Samuel Alito, who wrote the majority opinion in an asylum case, appeared to rebut Justice Sonia Sotomayor, who wrote the dissent. The move was highly unusual — and on Friday there was a coda.

(Image credit: Alex Wong)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: It's not just Cape Verde: African soccer is having a World Cup moment
item.description: The soccer greats — Brazil, England, Spain — have been giving uneven performances out on the pitch during this World Cup. Meanwhile, teams from Africa have been relentless.
item.content:encoded:
The soccer greats — Brazil, England, Spain — have been giving uneven performances out on the pitch during this World Cup. Meanwhile, teams from Africa have been relentless.

(Image credit: Carolina Herrera for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Garsd

item.title: She posted about ICE. Five months later, DHS agents told her to take her post down
item.description: In January, Paigelynne Gonyea posted about the immigration surge in Minneapolis. This week, she was visited by ICE officials who claimed one of her posts had doxxed a federal agent.
item.content:encoded:
In January, Paigelynne Gonyea posted about the immigration surge in Minneapolis. This week, she was visited by ICE officials who claimed one of her posts had doxxed a federal agent.

(Image credit: Gracious Golden via Facebook/Screenshot by NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block

item.title: U.S. pledges generous earthquake relief to Venezuela
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio promised: "We have a whole-of-government response. It'll be big; it'll be fast; and it'll be effective."
item.content:encoded:
Secretary of State Marco Rubio promised: "We have a whole-of-government response. It'll be big; it'll be fast; and it'll be effective."

(Image credit: Federico Parra/AFP)
item.dc:creator: Fatma Tanis

item.title: World Cup fans are missing games after their resale tickets fall through
item.description: The ordeal has left fans forced to either miss out on a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity or scramble to find new tickets, often costing more and for worse seats.
item.content:encoded:
The ordeal has left fans forced to either miss out on a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity or scramble to find new tickets, often costing more and for worse seats.

(Image credit: Shafkat Anowar)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: Palestinians grieve for a father-to-be shot by Israeli troops the day his son was born
item.description: Nayef Samaro, 25, was killed in an Israeli military raid on Nablus, in the occupied West Bank, as he prepared to meet his wife at the hospital for the birth of their first child.
item.content:encoded:
Nayef Samaro, 25, was killed in an Israeli military raid on Nablus, in the occupied West Bank, as he prepared to meet his wife at the hospital for the birth of their first child.

(Image credit: Ruth Sherlock)
item.dc:creator: Ruth Sherlock

item.title: Ukraine unleashes one of its heaviest drone bombardments, as Russia strikes Ukraine
item.description: Ukraine launched a major nighttime attack on a dozen Russian regions, Russian-held Crimea and the surrounding seas, and Russia attacked various regions of Ukraine, as their war grinds on.
item.content:encoded:
Ukraine launched a major nighttime attack on a dozen Russian regions, Russian-held Crimea and the surrounding seas, and Russia attacked various regions of Ukraine, as their war grinds on.

(Image credit: Kateryna Klochko)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
